headline: FTAI Aviation (FTAI): 3 Reasons We Love This Stock
summary: The past six months have been a windfall for FTAI Aviation’s shareholders. The company’s stock price has jumped 53.5%, hitting $251.77 per share. This performance may have investors wondering how to approach the situation.

headline: BA vs. LMT: Which Aerospace-Defense Giant Offers Better Value Today?
summary: Both Boeing and Lockheed Martin benefit from significant exposure to military aircraft, space systems and government defense contracts.

headline: Where Will Boeing Stock Be in 10 Years?
summary: Few dispute that Boeing will have a new narrow-body in place over the next decade, but the question is in what form and what it will take to get there.

headline: Saudi Arabia pushes ahead with launch of new airline despite Iran war
summary: The biggest airline start-up this century is forging ahead with plans to launch in Saudi Arabia despite disruption caused by the Iran war. Riyadh Air will collect its first new jets from Boeing this week before commencing flights on July 1, including to London. Tony Douglas, the carrier’s British chief executive, said an extra 22 routes including will be unveiled in quick succession as part of a plan to serve 100 cities worldwide within five years and establish a fleet of more than 180 jets.
